news.summary: Orange Logic, a digital asset management and content orchestration leader, has named Ben Colenso as Managing Director for Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (EMEA). Colenso, based in the UK, will oversee regional strategy, customer enablement, and market expansion. With 20 years of experience in technology and media production, including leading technology strategy for Hogarth, Colenso's appointment comes as Orange Logic seeks to expand its global presence.
news.headline: Orange Logic appoints Ben Colenso as Managing Director for EMEA. Colenso to lead regional strategy with 20 years experience.

news.summary: A technology-focused market intelligence, advisory, and research firm, the Futurum Group, has introduced its newest division, Futurum Equities Research, based in Austin, Texas. The division will be overseen by its newly-named Chief Market Strategist Shay Boloor. Boloor's responsibilities will involve leading the development of daily morning market notes by Futurum Equities, which are currently shared with more than 7,000 individuals in addition to the firm's existing clients and subscribers.
news.headline: Futurum Group introduces new division, Futurum Equities Research. Shay Boloor appointed Chief Market Strategist, reaching 7,000 recipients.

news.summary: In Tucson, Arizona on June 5, 2025, ResiXperts, a prominent residential services company supported by FoW Partners, has revealed its collaboration with Temco Air Environmental, a top cooling and heating service provider. The partnership combines the strengths of both companies to enhance their services and expand their reach in the local market.
news.headline: ResiXperts partners with Temco Air Environmental in Tucson to expand in Arizona, backed by FoW Partners.

news.summary: Salt Lake City, June 5, 2025 - Banner Capital Management, LLC (Banner) has successfully completed a portfolio recapitalization into Banner Capital Fund I (Fund I) and unveiled the launch of Banner Capital Fund II (Fund II). Fund I, a multi-asset continuation, marks a significant milestone for Banner as they progress with their investment strategy.
news.headline: Banner Capital Management recaps Fund I and launches Fund II on June 5, 2025. Portfolio recap leads to Fund I's closing.

news.summary: A healthcare professional, Tara Knight, DNP, FNP-C, has been awarded the prestigious 2025 Robert F. Gagel, M.D. Community Leadership Award by the Bone Health and Osteoporosis Foundation (BHOF) in Arlington, Va. This award honors individuals for their outstanding contributions to the community.
news.headline: Tara Knight, DNP, FNP-C, wins 2025 Robert F. Gagel, M.D. Community Leadership Award for healthcare initiatives.

news.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell's position is under scrutiny due to ongoing conflict with President Donald Trump, who believes Powell and the Fed are not cutting rates quickly enough. Trump has even considered firing Powell, although legally challenged, leading to uncertainty in the stock market in 2025. A recent Benzinga poll showed that 66% of readers believe Powell should serve out the remainder of his term, while 21% believe he should resign. This conflict raises questions about Powell's future in the role through May 2028. Powell has emphasized the independence of the Fed and stated that monetary policy will depend on economic factors.
news.headline: Trump and Powell's conflict raises questions about Powell's role. Only 21% say he should resign, 66% want him to serve out his term.

news.summary: In Charleston, South Carolina, on June 5th, 2025, Newtide introduced a cutting-edge enterprise AI platform called RisingTide. This platform is tailored for the fuels and convenience retail sector, making it the first of its kind in the market. RisingTide aims to revolutionize agentic automation with the support of Data Helm, Agent Harbor, and Shipyard. This innovative solution is set to transform the industry landscape by offering advanced capabilities.
news.headline: Newtide launches enterprise AI platform 'RisingTide' for fuels and retail industry. CHARLESTON, S.C., June 5, 2025.

news.summary: Don't miss out on "View The Experience" happening in Las Vegas on June 5, 2025. FLO Advertising, known for their mobile digital billboard innovation, has teamed up with HYPERVSN, experts in immersive 3D holographic technology, and FIREFLY, the top company in mobility-based advertising. This collaboration promises to deliver a cutting-edge experience. Be sure to check it out.
news.headline: FLO Advertising, HYPERVSN, and FIREFLY introduce world's first 3D holographic experience truck on Las Vegas Strip, available nationwide through FIREFLY's media network.

news.summary: In Santa Ana, California on June 5, 2025, Autonomous Medical Devices Incorporated (AMDI) confirmed the completion of a multi-center clinical trial endorsing the FDA application for its Mini Respiratory Panel. The company specializes in rapid PCR-based diagnostics and is on track for regulatory approval. This milestone marks a significant achievement for AMDI in advancing its technology.
news.headline: AMDI finishes clinical study for sub 10-min fast PCR system, gets MDSAP cert, FDA submission for Mini Respiratory Panel.

news.summary: In Portlaoise, Ireland on June 5, 2025, a tribute is being paid to the late legendary actor and vocalist Sir Christopher Lee on the 10th anniversary of his passing. Composer Damien Cain, hailing from Ireland, has reintroduced a hidden treasure to commemorate the occasion. The collaboration between Cain and Sir Christopher Lee, titled 'Elenore', was originally produced in 2006 and has been brought back to the public eye. This special release holds sentimental value as it reflects on the talent and legacy of Sir Christopher Lee, aiming to keep his memory alive through music.
news.headline: "Christopher Lee's 'Elenore' recording rediscovered after 10 years, re-released to mark his passing. - PRNewswire. #ChristopherLee #Elenore"

news.summary: The launch of HireRight's 2025 Global Benchmark Report was recently announced in Nashville, Tennessee. This report presents key data and insights for businesses to stay competitive in the industry.
news.headline: HireRight releases 2025 Global Benchmark Report in Nashville, announcing key industry trends and insights.

news.summary: The global Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) market is experiencing rapid growth, with a value of around USD 242.80 billion in 2024, expected to reach USD 276.85 billion in 2025 and projected to reach USD 1061.49 billion by 2034, at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of about 16.1% during the forecast period 2025 to 2034. Major players like Medtronic, GE Healthcare, Philips, Boston Scientific, and Johnson & Johnson are driving advancements in smart devices and AI-powered diagnostics, focusing on interoperable and secure platforms for optimized data exchange in healthcare value chains. The market growth is fueled by increased demand for remote patient monitoring, advancements in wireless technology (5G networks, low-power communication protocols), and integration with AI and Big Data analytics. These technologies enable real-time monitoring, predictive analytics, personalized treatment plans, early disease detection, and proactive healthcare management, contributing to improved patient outcomes. The report covers market size, growth factors, key segments, regional scope, competitive landscape, and recent trends, offering insights into the evolving IoMT market landscape.
news.headline: Global Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) market projected to reach USD 1061.49 Billion by 2034, with a 16.1% CAGR. Key players include Medtronic, GE Healthcare, and Philips. Increased demand for remote patient monitoring and advancements in wireless technology driving market growth. Integrating AI and Big Data into IoMT systems is transforming healthcare delivery.

news.summary: In June 5, 2025, in Bronx, New York, Albert Einstein College of Medicine announced the appointment of Amanda Raff, M.D. '98, as the new senior associate dean for medical education. Dr. Raff, a distinguished professor of medicine and also serving as the associate chair of medicine for undergraduate medical education, brings her expertise and experience to this role.
news.headline: Amanda Raff, M.D. '98, named Senior Associate Dean for Medical Education at Albert Einstein College of Medicine.

news.summary: Inflows from institutions and increasing Ethereum transaction fees stand in contrast to low network activity and hesitancy in the Ether futures market. The Ethereum network has seen a rise in institutional investments, with a significant increase in transaction fees. However, general network activity remains slow, and there is a sense of caution among investors in the Ether futures market.
news.headline: Ethereum's potential $3K price may depend on institutional inflows and rising transaction fees, amid slow network activity and future market caution.

news.summary: Armacell, a company based in Chapel Hill, North Carolina, has recently named JR Turner as the Vice President for the Americas region. This move signals a strategic decision to strengthen leadership in the region and drive growth. Turner will be responsible for overseeing operations across North and South America and is expected to bring his expertise to further enhance the company's performance in the region. This appointment comes as part of Armacell's efforts to expand its presence in the Americas and capitalize on the potential for growth in these markets.
news.headline: Armacell appoints JR Turner as new Vice President - AMERICAS in Chapel Hill, N.C.

news.summary: In Houston on June 5, 2025, the intellectual property firm, Heim, Payne & Chorush, LLP, was acknowledged in the Chambers USA guide as a leading firm in Texas for technology cases, particularly in antitrust claims relating to patents and trade secret disputes. Known for its expertise in the field, the firm's recognition highlights their standing in the legal community. This accolade underscores their strong performance and reputation, establishing them as a go-to choice for clients facing intellectual property challenges.
news.headline: Houston IP firm Heim, Payne & Chorush recognized in Chambers USA 2025 rankings for tech cases in Texas.
